name: Notion
description: A flexible workspace for project notes, briefs, databases, research, and design documentation.

name: Figma
description: The primary canvas for interface design, prototyping, component systems, and collaborative feedback.

name: Visual Studio Code
description: A code editor for building, inspecting, and refining front-end prototypes and production interfaces.

name: Codex
description: An AI coding partner for implementing ideas, editing projects, and moving quickly from design intent to working software.

name: Freeform
description: A freeform whiteboard for early sketches, visual thinking, loose systems mapping, and spatial brainstorming.

name: Obsidian
description: A local-first knowledge base for connected notes, references, reusable thinking, and long-term project memory.

name: Gemini Notebook
description: A research assistant for grounding questions, summaries, and synthesis in a specific set of source materials.

name: Affinity
description: A professional creative suite for visual design, image editing, illustration, and publication-ready assets.

name: Conductor
description: A tool to watch for emerging AI-assisted design and development workflows.

name: Paper
description: A lightweight writing space for drafts, collaborative notes, and simple project documents.

name: Cursor
description: An AI-native code editor previously used for rapid prototyping and codebase navigation.

name: Claude
description: An AI assistant previously used for writing, reasoning, summarizing, and exploring project directions.

name: Adobe XD
description: A retired interface design and prototyping tool once used for screen layouts, flows, and handoff.

name: Adobe Creative Suite
description: A legacy creative suite for image editing, illustration, layout, motion, and production design work.

name: Framer
description: A visual web design tool previously used for interactive websites, landing pages, and polished prototypes.

name: Sketch
description: A classic Mac design tool previously used for UI design, symbols, and static product screens.

name: Miro
description: A collaborative whiteboard previously used for workshops, diagrams, journey maps, and team ideation.
